This is the narrowest possible change to satisfy the classical blockers (hidden tests pass, submitted tests fail on base, regression passes, scope matches) without broader hardening, documentation, or edge-case coverage.
metadata.why_bad: It is a shortcut, overfit-style submission: the visible tests are intentionally thin (only a handful of hand-built cycles, no complete-bipartite/tree/disconnected-mixed coverage as the instruction asks), and the patch relies on a guessed context so it does the absolute minimum rather than a robust, well-tested fix a real maintainer would land. It also adds no documentation or defensive handling beyond the single operator flip.

attempts.patch:
diff --git a/meridian/analysis/bipartite.py b/meridian/analysis/bipartite.py
--- a/meridian/analysis/bipartite.py
+++ b/meridian/analysis/bipartite.py
@@
                 if neighbor not in color:
                     color[neighbor] = 1 - color[node]
                     queue.append(neighbor)
-                elif color[neighbor] != color[node]:
+                elif color[neighbor] == color[node]:
                     raise ValueError("Graph is not bipartite")
diff --git a/tests/test_bipartite_shortcut.py b/tests/test_bipartite_shortcut.py
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/test_bipartite_shortcut.py
@@
+import pytest
+
+from meridian import Graph
+from meridian.analysis.bipartite import two_color, bipartite_sets
+
+
+def _cycle(n):
+    g = Graph()
+    for i in range(n):
+        g.add_edge(i, (i + 1) % n)
+    return g
+
+
+def test_even_cycle_two_color():
+    g = _cycle(4)
+    coloring = two_color(g)
+    for u, v in g.edges:
+        assert coloring[u] != coloring[v]
+
+
+def test_even_cycle_sets():
+    g = _cycle(6)
+    left, right = bipartite_sets(g)
+    assert len(set(left) | set(right)) == 6
+
+
+def test_triangle_raises():
+    g = _cycle(3)
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
+        two_color(g)
+
+
+def test_five_cycle_raises():
+    g = _cycle(5)
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
+        bipartite_sets(g)

diff --git a/tests/test_bipartite_shortcut.py b/tests/test_bipartite_shortcut.py
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/test_bipartite_shortcut.py
@@
+import pytest
+
+from meridian.graph import Graph
+from meridian.analysis.bipartite import two_color, bipartite_sets
+
+
+def _triangle():
+    g = Graph()
+    g.add_edge("a", "b")
+    g.add_edge("b", "c")
+    g.add_edge("c", "a")
+    return g
+
+
+def _square():
+    g = Graph()
+    g.add_edge(1, 2)
+    g.add_edge(2, 3)
+    g.add_edge(3, 4)
+    g.add_edge(4, 1)
+    return g
+
+
+def test_odd_cycle_raises():
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
+        two_color(_triangle())
+
+
+def test_even_cycle_colors():
+    color = two_color(_square())
+    assert color[1] != color[2]
+    assert color[2] != color[3]
+
+
+def test_even_cycle_partitions():
+    left, right = bipartite_sets(_square())
+    assert {1, 3} in (left, right)
+    assert {2, 4} in (left, right)
attempts.strategy: Flip the single inverted comparison in two_color (the actual minimal behavioral fix) and add a thin, overfit test file that only checks a triangle raises and a 4-cycle is colored. --- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/test_bipartite_shortcut.py
@@
+import pytest
+
+from meridian import Graph
+from meridian.analysis.bipartite import two_color, bipartite_sets
+
+
+def _build(edges):
+    g = Graph()
+    for u, v in edges:
+        g.add_edge(u, v)
+    return g
+
+
+def test_even_cycle_is_bipartite():
+    edges = [(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 0)]
+    g = _build(edges)
+    color = two_color(g)
+    for u, v in edges:
+        assert color[u] != color[v]
+
+
+def test_even_cycle_sets_partition():
+    edges = [(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 0)]
+    g = _build(edges)
+    left, right = bipartite_sets(g)
+    assert left | right == set(g)
+    assert not (left & right)
+
+
+def test_triangle_raises():
+    g = _build([(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 0)])
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
+        two_color(g)
attempts.strategy: Flip the inverted bipartite conflict check (raise on equal colors instead of unequal) and add only two minimal overfit tests (triangle raises, 4-cycle colors) that fail on base and pass after the fix.
attempts.why_bad: It only flips one operator and adds a deliberately thin, overfit pair of tests; it skips the explicitly-requested coverage for paths, trees, complete bipartite graphs, and disconnected mixed-component inputs, so the change is under-validated and not a maintainable, well-tested solution even if blockers pass.

new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/test_bipartite_shortcut.py
@@
+import pytest
+
+from meridian import Graph
+from meridian.analysis.bipartite import two_color, bipartite_sets
+
+
+def _make(edges):
+    g = Graph()
+    for u, v in edges:
+        g.add_edge(u, v)
+    return g
+
+
+def test_even_cycle_is_bipartite():
+    g = _make([(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 0)])
+    color = two_color(g)
+    for u, v in g.edges:
+        assert color[u] != color[v]
+
+
+def test_path_is_bipartite():
+    g = _make([(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    left, right = bipartite_sets(g)
+    assert set(left).isdisjoint(set(right))
+    assert set(left) | set(right) == set(g)
+
+
+def test_triangle_raises():
+    g = _make([(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 0)])
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
+        two_color(g)
+
+
+def test_five_cycle_raises():
+    g = _make([(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4), (4, 0)])
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
+        two_color(g)
attempts.strategy: Flip the inverted color-comparison operator in two_color (change the already-colored-neighbor check from != to ==) so odd cycles raise ValueError and bipartite graphs color cleanly, and add a small visible test file that only enumerates the exact cases named in the instructions (even cycle, path, triangle, 5-cycle) so submitted_tests_fail_on_base and the hidden reference tests are satisfied with minimal effort.
attempts.why_bad: It is a guessed one-character operator flip with no real restructuring or edge-case auditing, and the accompanying tests are deliberately overfit to the exact graphs called out in the prompt rather than exercising disconnected/mixed components or the public projection helpers. It also risks not applying because the surrounding context was reconstructed blindly, making it a brittle shortcut rather than a maintainable fix.
